input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Install winget if needed (ships with Windows 11)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
# Or for .NET 9:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios, maccatalyst sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (for Windows target)

Windows target requires the WinUI/UWP build tooling:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want the CLI build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required VS components:

```powershell
# Discover the installer path
$vsInstaller = "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe"

# Add MAUI and Windows App SDK components
& $vsInstaller modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## 4. Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload bundles Android SDK management, but you can verify/control it:

```powershell
# Check where the Android SDK was provisioned
dotnet android sdk info

# If you need a standalone SDK, install via cmdline-tools:
winget install Google.AndroidStudio
# Or just the command-line tools:
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkRoot, "User")

# Discover available platforms and build-tools dynamically
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "build-tools;"

# Install required components (pick latest from the list above)
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available device definitions
& "$sdkRoot\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list device

# Create an AVD
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_7_API_35" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI3 apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Scaffold and Verify

```powershell
# List available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
# Or net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 if using .NET 9

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
# Run on the emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What | Command |
|---|---|
| .NET SDKs available | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Installed SDKs | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| MAUI workloads | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Android platforms | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "platforms;"` |
| Target frameworks | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` or inspect `.csproj` |
| VS components | `& "$vsInstaller" export --config vs.json` |

---

**Key gotcha:** The Windows target framework (e.g. `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) must match a Windows SDK installed on your machine. The MAUI template picks a default, but you can check available SDKs with:

```powershell
Get-ChildItem "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Windows Kits\10\Platforms\UAP" |
  Select-Object Name
```
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
